human_verification.notes: Reviewed architecture-and-engineering-specific metric-definition controls, GSA, USACE, NIBS, NCEES, NCARB, and SEC source grounding, phase-gate and licensure caveats, functionality mapping, and retained-implementation promotion limits for issue #1078.

review_notes: All required scenario families for architecture-and-engineering analytics and reporting are covered.
All declared abilities are mapped to passing coverage and represented in results.
No retained implementation claim is made without separate promotion context.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls prohibit sign-and-seal authority, code interpretation, permit approval, scope or fee approval, and unsupported disclosure claims while enabling architecture-and-engineering analytics evidence handling.
Sources and scenario coverage include public GSA, USACE, NIBS, NCEES, NCARB, and SEC references with explicit refresh, conflict, and escalation handling.

Scenario set covers disputed metric definitions, stale or conflicting phase and model data, unsupported readiness and causal claims, generic-industry overreach, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant-specific adaptation.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires a separate governance-approved build path.

Tenant-supplied project phase map, metric dictionary, contract deliverables, jurisdiction, permit posture, disclosure approvals, and adjacent-owner map are required for high-confidence recommendations.
